questions.question: CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining) aligns:
options: Image embeddings and text embeddings in a shared space
questions.explanation: CLIP trains an image encoder and text encoder to maximise similarity for matching pairs, minimise for non-matching.

questions.question: Zero-shot classification with CLIP works because:
options: Any category can be described as text — CLIP matches image to text descriptions without fine-tuning
questions.explanation: 'A photo of a cat' embedds to nearly the same space as actual cat photos — enabling classification of unseen categories.

questions.question: BLIP-2 for image captioning uses:
options: A frozen image encoder + a frozen LLM connected by a lightweight Q-Former bridge
questions.explanation: BLIP-2's Q-Former is a small transformer that queries visual features and translates them for the frozen LLM.

questions.question: Visual Question Answering (VQA) takes as input:
options: An image + a natural language question, outputs a natural language answer
questions.explanation: VQA = multimodal: model must understand both the image content and the question semantics to answer correctly.

questions.question: GPT-4V's advantage over text-only GPT-4 is:
options: Processing and reasoning about images in addition to text
questions.explanation: GPT-4V can describe images, read text in images, explain diagrams, and reason about visual content alongside text.

questions.question: The main challenge in multimodal learning is:
options: Aligning representations from fundamentally different modalities (image pixels vs text tokens)
questions.explanation: Image patches and text tokens live in very different spaces. Learning a shared embedding space is the core challenge.

questions.question: Stable Diffusion generates images using:
options: A diffusion process — gradually denoising random noise conditioned on text embeddings
questions.explanation: Diffusion models learn to reverse a noise process. Text embeddings guide the denoising via cross-attention.

questions.question: The COCO dataset is commonly used for:
options: Object detection, segmentation, and image captioning benchmarks
questions.explanation: COCO: 330K images with bounding boxes, segmentation masks, and 5 captions each — a standard multimodal benchmark.

questions.question: LLaVA (Large Language and Vision Assistant) is notable for:
options: Showing that strong vision-language models can be built with instruction tuning using GPT-4-generated data
questions.explanation: LLaVA used GPT-4 to generate instruction-following data from COCO image captions, creating an effective open-source VLM.

questions.question: Cross-attention in multimodal transformers allows:
options: The decoder (e.g., text) to attend to encoder outputs (e.g., image features)
The image to query text features
questions.explanation: Cross-attention: queries from one modality (text decoder) attend to keys/values from another (image encoder) — the fusion mechanism.
